name: Ironbelly's Meats & Ratcatching
description: A narrow shop squeezed between two larger buildings on the market square, identifiable by the hooks outside the door and the perpetual smell of blood, salt, and sawdust. Durgin Ironbelly runs it — 'runs' being generous. He butchers adequately. He is the only butcher in Millhaven, which is the sole reason Millhaven has a butcher. The front room displays hanging meats, cured sausages, and whatever Durgin managed to process that morning. The back room is where the actual work happens and where Durgin's ratcatching equipment is stored — his other job, and the one he's marginally better at. His wife sends him supplies and instructions by letter from the city. He follows about half of them.

sounds: The thwack of a cleaver — slightly off-rhythm
The scrape of sawdust being swept
A customer haggling, which Durgin always loses
smells: Sawdust on the floor — fresh every morning, Durgin's one reliable habit
Salt brine from the curing barrels
Durgin himself, after a morning's work
Honeycakes — he always has one nearby
atmosphere.lighting: A single front window and the open door. The back room is lit by a single oil lamp. Durgin doesn't notice the dimness.

layout.name: The Shop Front
layout.description: Hooks, a counter, a scale that Durgin forgets to calibrate. Hanging meats and sausage links. A basket of honeycakes that are not for sale but Durgin eats from constantly.

layout.name: The Back Room
layout.description: The butchering workspace. A heavy block, knives of varying sharpness, and curing barrels. Also where Durgin stores his ratcatching gear — nets, cages, and a club named 'Persuader.'

layout.name: The Cold Cellar
layout.description: Below the shop. Keeps meat fresh longer in summer. Durgin is afraid of the dark but won't admit it, so trips to the cellar are fast and noisy.

hooks: Bobbert the Gleeful buys Durgin's 'reject cuts' at a discount and turns them into tavern stew — the arrangement benefits everyone
Elara Sharpwit drinks at the Bottoms Up and picks up work as a hired blade — she buys jerky in bulk and never explains where she's headed
The rats in Millhaven's sewers have been getting larger and smarter — Durgin's traps aren't working as well as they used to
Durgin's wife sent a letter threatening to visit — he has been cleaning the shop with an urgency the town has never seen

worldContext.location: Ironbelly's Meats & Ratcatching, Market Square, Millhaven
